TORNADO. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED AS A SECOND TOUCHDOWN FROM THE HOBE SOUND TORNADO THAT DID DAMAGE TO SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES IN THE AREA. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 0.39 MILES 40 YARDS WIDE RATING EF-0 WITH MAX WINDS 65 TO 75 MPH. WIND DAMGAGE TO NUMEROUS TREES OCCURRED THROUGH THE LOBLOLLY BAY RESIDENTIAL COMMUNITY.

THE SECOND TOUCH DOWN OF THE HOBE SOUND TORNADO WAS REPORTED BY A MARINER TO HAVE MOVED ACROSS PECK LAKE AS A WATERSPOUT. A WIND VANE ATOP A 55 FOOT MAST ON A BOAT IN THE LAKE MEASURED WIND GUSTS NEAR 65 KNOTS. THE VESSEL EXPERIENCED A BROKEN PORT HOLE AND FOUR NEARBY BOATS SUSTAINED A TORN CANVAS WITH ONE OF THESE SHIPS BEING PUSHED AGROUND BY THE WIND.

TORNADO. PATH LENGTH 0.1 MILES 25 YARDS WIDE RATING EF-0 WITH MAX WINDS 65 TO 70 MPH REMOVED A ROOF FROM MOBILE HOME AND BLEW IT 50 YARDS. SEVERAL HOMES IN THE AREA HAD MINOR DAMAGE. A LARGE PALM TREE WAS UPROOTED AND MANY SMALL TREES WERE DOWNED IN THE SAME LOCATION.
SPOTTER REPORTED WATERSPOUT SEEN FROM JENSEN BEACH. KFPR OBSERVER ALSO REPORTED WATERSPOUT.
